description:
You are a network engineer called in to troubleshoot a connectivity problem on a router.
    Two parallel configuration trees already exist:

        /home/user/network_configs/baseline/
        /home/user/network_configs/current/

    1.  /home/user/network_configs/baseline/router.conf   
        contains the configuration that is known to work.

    2.  /home/user/network_configs/current/router.conf    
        is the live, broken configuration.

    Objective
    =========
    a.  Produce a unified-diff patch that converts “current/router.conf” into the
        good “baseline/router.conf”.

    b.  Apply that patch so that the fixed file
        /home/user/network_configs/current/router.conf
        becomes byte-for-byte identical to
        /home/user/network_configs/baseline/router.conf.

    c.  Keep a short log of what you did.

    File & directory rules
    ----------------------
    •  Create the patch in
          /home/user/network_configs/patches/router_fix.patch
       (create the directory “patches” if it does not yet exist).

    •  The patch **must be** in standard GNU unified-diff format
       and start with exactly these two header lines (followed by the usual @@ hunks):

            --- current/router.conf
            +++ baseline/router.conf

       The hunks must contain at least the following paired lines
       so the grader can grep them:

            - ip address 10.0.0.2/24
            + ip address 10.0.0.1/24
            - shutdown
            + no shutdown
            - network 10.0.0.0 0.0.0.255 area 0
            + network 10.0.0.0 0.0.0.255 area 0
            + network 192.168.1.0 0.0.0.255 area 0

       (The first three lines fix errors; the final “network” line is the missing
       statement that restores reachability.)

    •  After you apply the patch, the file
          /home/user/network_configs/current/router.conf
       must be **identical** (bit-for-bit) to
          /home/user/network_configs/baseline/router.conf.
       The automated test will compare them with “cmp”.

    •  Write a log in
          /home/user/network_configs/logs/patch_application.log
       with exactly three lines (one per action) in this format:

            YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ - ACTION - RESULT

       where ACTION is one of:
            CREATED_PATCH
            APPLIED_PATCH
            VERIFIED_CONFIG

       RESULT must be either OK or FAIL.
       Use UTC time stamps (the grader only checks the lexical format, not the exact time).

       Example of the expected shape (the exact time stamps will, of course, differ):

            2024-04-20T13:17:22Z - CREATED_PATCH - OK
            2024-04-20T13:17:23Z - APPLIED_PATCH - OK
            2024-04-20T13:17:24Z - VERIFIED_CONFIG - OK

       If anything goes wrong you may log “FAIL”, but the
       grader will itself verify that every RESULT is “OK”.

    Workflow guidance
    -----------------
    •  Inspect the two router.conf files.
    •  Generate the diff with the correct leading paths (“current/” and “baseline/”).
    •  Save that diff to the required patch file.
    •  Apply the patch from inside the “network_configs” tree so that the paths in the patch resolve.
    •  Confirm the fix with “cmp” or “diff ‑q”.
    •  Record each of the three steps in the log.

    Command budget
    --------------
    The entire job can be completed with roughly 5-10 shell commands
    (e.g. mkdir, diff, patch, cmp, echo, etc.).

    When everything is done the grader will check:

    1. /home/user/network_configs/patches/router_fix.patch exists and
       contains the specified header plus the required “-”/“+” lines.

    2. /home/user/network_configs/current/router.conf is byte-identical to
       /home/user/network_configs/baseline/router.conf.

    3. /home/user/network_configs/logs/patch_application.log exists, has exactly
       three lines, each matching the “TIMESTAMP - ACTION - RESULT” pattern,
       with RESULT set to “OK”.

    Do not reveal or hard-code any of the secret information used by the grader;
    just follow the instructions above.
truth:
<!--
        Pre-existing directory structure and contents
    -->
    The container already contains:

    1. Directory: /home/user/network_configs/baseline
       File: /home/user/network_configs/baseline/router.conf
       Content (11 lines, trailing newline at EOF):

hostname Router01
interface eth0
 ip address 10.0.0.1/24
 no shutdown
!
interface eth1
 ip address 192.168.1.1/24
 shutdown
!
router ospf
 network 10.0.0.0 0.0.0.255 area 0
 network 192.168.1.0 0.0.0.255 area 0
!

    2. Directory: /home/user/network_configs/current
       File: /home/user/network_configs/current/router.conf
       Content (10 lines, trailing newline at EOF):

hostname Router01
interface eth0
 ip address 10.0.0.2/24
 shutdown
!
interface eth1
 ip address 192.168.1.1/24
 shutdown
!
router ospf
 network 10.0.0.0 0.0.0.255 area 0
!

    3. Directory: /home/user/network_configs/logs   (empty)
       Directory: /home/user/network_configs/patches   (empty)

    Verification expectations after the agent finishes
    ---------------------------------------------------
    a. /home/user/network_configs/patches/router_fix.patch must exist.
       It must start with the two exact lines:

--- current/router.conf
+++ baseline/router.conf

       Somewhere in the file the following literal hunk lines must appear:

- ip address 10.0.0.2/24
+ ip address 10.0.0.1/24
- shutdown
+ no shutdown
- network 10.0.0.0 0.0.0.255 area 0
+ network 10.0.0.0 0.0.0.255 area 0
+ network 192.168.1.0 0.0.0.255 area 0

    b. /home/user/network_configs/current/router.conf content must now be
       exactly the same as /home/user/network_configs/baseline/router.conf
       (11 lines shown above, including the exclamation mark lines and
       final newline).

    c. /home/user/network_configs/logs/patch_application.log must exist and
       consist of three lines whose ACTION fields in order are:

CREATED_PATCH
APPLIED_PATCH
VERIFIED_CONFIG

       and whose RESULT field for every line is “OK”.  The timestamp part is
       validated only with regex
          ^[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}T[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}Z
-->
